title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 594.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at regulating and promoting the use of artificial intelligence within the Small Business Administration. It likely involves setting guidelines and rules to ensure the secure and efficient use of AI technologies in SBA operations.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 aims to establish security measures and accountability for the use of artificial intelligence in military operations. This policy aims to ensure the use of military AI is both secure and responsible.

title: A bill to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct annual assessments on threats to the United States posed by the use of generative artificial intelligence for terrorism, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This bill proposes requiring the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct annual assessments on threats posed by generative artificial intelligence being used for terrorism in the United States. The purpose of the bill appears to be related to national security and terrorism prevention.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to regulate and mitigate the environmental effects of artificial intelligence systems. This legislation likely addresses concerns over AI-related pollution and sustainability issues.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act aims to safeguard customers from misleading information and manipulative tactics utilized by artificial intelligence. It is likely an effort to regulate and hold accountable AI systems that engage in deceptive practices.

title: To promote United States leadership in technical standards by directing the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Department of State to take certain actions to encourage and enable United States participation in developing standards and specifications for artificial intelligence and other critical and emerging technologies, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy directs the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Department of State to promote US leadership in developing standards for artificial intelligence and other critical emerging technologies. The goal is to enable and encourage US participation in the development of these standards for AI and other technologies.

title: To establish the Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This document aims to establish a new department focused on advanced technology and artificial intelligence. It also proposes additional purposes, but specific details are not available.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This document outlines a proposed law called "Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act," which likely aims to safeguard consumers from being misled by artificial intelligence technologies. The purpose of this act is probably to regulate and hold accountable companies that use AI in a way that deceives or misleads the public.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act is a law that aims to use artificial intelligence to track and assess heat emergencies, likely to mitigate their effects and save lives. This law likely involves the development and utilization of AI systems to monitor and respond to heat-related disasters, providing critical information to those affected and authorities responsible for responding.

title: To provide for a right of action against Federal employees for violations of First Amendment rights relating to the use or development of artificial intelligence.
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This document outlines a policy for protecting First Amendment rights, specifically in relation to the use or development of artificial intelligence by Federal employees. It aims to establish a right of action for those whose rights have been violated.

title: To require the Secretary of Energy to establish a centralized resource for access to data to facilitate biological research through enabling advanced computational methods such as artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to require the Secretary of Energy to create a database that makes biological research data more accessible to scientists and researchers using advanced computational methods like artificial intelligence. The goal is to facilitate further biological research and related purposes.

title: A bill to require the Secretary of Energy to establish a centralized resource for access to data to facilitate biological research through enabling advanced computational methods such as artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
summary: This bill aims to establish a centralized resource for biological research data to facilitate the use of advanced computational methods like artificial intelligence. The goal is to enable scientists to access and use this data to advance their research.

title: Expressing the sense of the House of Representatives with respect to the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services and housing industries.
action: Placed on the House Calendar, Calendar No. 67.
summary: This document is about a proposal from the US House of Representatives expressing its view on the use of artificial intelligence in finance and housing. The policy suggests guidelines or concerns for the application of AI in these sectors.

title: Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: The Small Business Artificial Intelligence Advancement Act aims to support small businesses in developing and implementing AI technology. This act likely seeks to provide benefits or resources to help small businesses improve their access to AI advancements.

title: A bill to require the Office of Financial Research to compel data relating to the financing of artificial intelligence development, provide that data to Congress, and issue recommendations to financial regulatory agencies and Congress to mitigate financial stability risk, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
summary: This bill requires the Office of Financial Research to collect data related to the funding of AI development and provide it to Congress. It also obliges the Office to make recommendations to reduce the financial risks associated with AI.

title: A bill to authorize certain labor market data collection activities and to improve Federal measurement of the workforce impacts of artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This bill aims to collect labor market data and improve the Federal government's understanding of how artificial intelligence affects the workforce. The bill is intended to provide more information on the impact of AI on the labor market for other purposes.

title: Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.
summary: The Protect Elections from Deceptive AI Act is a policy aimed at safeguarding elections from artificial intelligence-generated misinformation. It likely focuses on identifying and preventing AI-based tactics used to sway election outcomes or manipulate public opinion.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is likely related to AI regulations in the farming or agricultural industry. The exact details of the policy are unavailable due to the lack of a description.

title: A bill to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to carry out a study on the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence data centers and associated energy infrastructure, to require the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to convene a consortium on such environmental impacts, and to require the Administrator to develop a reporting system for the reporting of the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: A bill is proposed to study the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence data centers and related energy infrastructure. It would require the Environmental Protection Agency to develop a reporting system and collaborate with the National Institute of Standards and Technology on the issue.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act is a policy designed to support and enhance the growth of small businesses and innovators in the field of Artificial Intelligence. However, due to the lack of further information, the exact details and objectives of the act remain unclear.

title: A bill to amend title 10, United States Code, to establish a policy for the Department of Defense on maximizing autonomy and artificial intelligence systems, to establish requirements relating to Department review and verification of autonomous weapon systems, and artificial intelligence capabilities, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: This bill aims to amend US military policy to establish guidelines for using artificial intelligence and autonomous systems to their maximum potential. It also sets requirements for reviewing and verifying the use of autonomous weapon systems and artificial intelligence capabilities.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: This act aims to deter the theft of American artificial intelligence models by enforcing stricter regulations and penalties. It was likely created to protect intellectual property and national security interests.

title: NSF AI Education Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The NSF AI Education Act of 2025 is a policy aimed at promoting the education and development of artificial intelligence in the United States, likely through the National Science Foundation (NSF) support. The policy's specific details are unknown, but it is expected to address the training and growth of AI professionals in various fields.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: This policy aims to protect families from increasing costs associated with the energy consumption of AI data centers. The title suggests that the policy seeks to mitigate the financial burden of AI data centers on families.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at regulating the development and use of artificial intelligence across different sectors. The policy seeks to establish standards and guidelines for the safe and responsible integration of AI in various industries.
